sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.21 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group,$p$an odd prime number, and$P$a Sylow$p$-sub\-group of$G$. Let the order of every non-identity normal subgroup of$G$be divisible by$p$. Suppose$P$has an element$x$that is conjugate to no other from$P$. Does$x$belong to the centre of$G$? For$p = 2$, the answer is positive (G.\,Glauberman, J.~Algebra, { 4} (1966), 403--420). { G.\,Glauberman} Yes, it does, mod CFSG (O.\,D.\,A...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
